title: Institutional Constellation Map
description: See how governments, companies, universities, and standards bodies connect across expertise, power, funding, and dependence.
note: Solid lines are knowledge; thick lines are authority; arrows are dependency; dashed lines are interface mechanisms.

nodes.label: AI Governance
nodes.description: Rules, standards, and oversight for how AI systems are built and deployed.
nodes.gap: Frontier knowledge sits in private labs and universities while formal authority sits, thinly, in public agencies.

nodes.label: Cybersecurity
nodes.description: Protection of digital systems and critical infrastructure from compromise.
nodes.gap: Authority and expertise both exist, but responsibility is split across many agencies and firms.

nodes.label: Military AI
nodes.description: Autonomy, decision support, and AI capability in defence settings.
nodes.gap: Defence holds the authority but depends on private labs for the underlying capability.

nodes.label: Data Governance
nodes.description: Control over how data is collected, shared, protected, and used.
nodes.gap: Broad public stakes with no clear owner and limited oversight of large private data systems.

nodes.label: Biosecurity
nodes.description: Managing risks from biological research, tools, and emerging capability.
nodes.gap: Expertise concentrated in universities and labs, with little standing authority to govern it.

nodes.label: NIST
nodes.description: A U.S. standards body that turns technical practice into measurable frameworks.
nodes.gap: Deep technical and standards weight, but little direct regulatory or deployment authority.

nodes.label: CISA
nodes.description: The U.S. civilian cybersecurity agency coordinating critical-infrastructure defence.
nodes.gap: Real operational authority in cyber, thinner reach into frontier AI expertise.

nodes.label: U.S. Department of Defense
nodes.description: The U.S. defence establishment, a dominant buyer and operator of technology.
nodes.gap: Enormous procurement and deployment authority that outruns in-house frontier expertise.

nodes.label: NATO DIANA
nodes.description: A NATO accelerator connecting emerging technology to collective defence.
nodes.gap: Convening and funding power without formal regulatory authority.

nodes.label: OpenAI
nodes.description: A private AI lab building frontier models and deployment infrastructure.
nodes.gap: Frontier capability and deployment reach with almost no formal public authority.

nodes.label: Anthropic
nodes.description: A private AI lab focused on frontier models and safety research.
nodes.gap: Safety and model expertise that sits outside any formal accountability structure.

nodes.label: Universities
nodes.description: The research base producing expertise, talent, and independent evaluation.
nodes.gap: The broadest knowledge base with the least authority to act on it.

edges.description: NIST holds technical and standards expertise for AI systems.
